name: Ginger Pork on Pineapple
recipeIngredient: Chopped onion (1/4 cup, about 1 small onion)
Green bell pepper, cut into 1-inch pieces (1 medium)
recipeInstructions:
1. Pare the pineapple and cut it crosswise into 6 slices, about 1 inch thick.
2. Spray a broiler pan rack with nonstick cooking spray. Arrange the pineapple slices on the rack in the broiler pan.
3. Cook the ground pork and onion in a 10-inch nonstick skillet over medium heat, stirring frequently, until the pork is light brown. Drain excess fat if necessary.
4. Stir in the remaining ingredients (brown sugar, vinegar, ginger, salt, pepper, bell pepper) into the pork mixture. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer uncovered for about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the bell pepper is crisp-tender.
5. Set the oven control to broil. Place the pineapple slices on a baking sheet under the broiler, approximately 4-5 inches from the heat source. Broil for 3-5 minutes, turning once, until they are hot and bubbly.
6. Serve the pork mixture over the broiled pineapple slices.
Microwave Instructions:
1. Arrange the pineapple slices in a deep, round microwavable platter.
2. Cover with waxed paper.
3. Crumble the cooked ground pork into a 2-quart microwavable casserole and add the onion.
4. Cover tightly and microwave on high for 6 to 8 minutes, stirring after 3 minutes until the pork is done; drain excess fat if necessary.
5. Stir in the remaining ingredients (brown sugar, vinegar, ginger, salt, pepper) into the pork mixture. Cover tightly and continue microwaving for an additional 8 to 10 minutes, stirring after 5 minutes, until the bell pepper is crisp-tender.
6. Let stand covered for 5 minutes before serving. Serve over the cooked pineapple slices.
